story_prompt: create a lecture on javascript function

scenes.text: In JavaScript, functions are fundamental building blocks. They allow you to encapsulate code and execute it wherever necessary. Let's start by understanding the three main types of function syntax in JavaScript.
scenes.image_prompt:
flowchart TD
  A[Function Declaration] --> B[Function Expression]
  B --> C[Arrow Function]

scenes.text: A function declaration is one way to define a function in JavaScript. It starts with the 'function' keyword followed by a unique name and is then invoked later in the program.
scenes.image_prompt:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> FunctionDeclaration
  FunctionDeclaration --> GlobalScope
  GlobalScope --> Usage
  Usage --> [*]

scenes.text: Function expressions allow you to define a function as part of an expression. These can be anonymous and are often used with callbacks.
scenes.image_prompt:
flowchart TD
  A[Create Function Expression] --> B[Assign to Variable]
  B --> C[Invoke Function]
  C --> D[End]

scenes.text: Arrow functions are a shorthand form of function expressions introduced in ES6. They provide a more concise syntax and handle the 'this' keyword differently.
scenes.image_prompt:
mindmap
  root((Arrow Function))
    Syntax
      Single-line
      Multi-line
    Behavior
      This Keyword
      Conciseness
    Use Cases

scenes.text: Understanding the scope of a function is crucial. JavaScript uses lexical scoping, meaning the accessibility of variables is determined by the position of functions and blocks in code.
scenes.image_prompt:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> GlobalScope
  GlobalScope --> FunctionScope
  FunctionScope --> LocalScope
  LocalScope --> [*]
